world.title: Xi to Meet Vietnam Leader as Hormuz Blockade Risks Energy Flows
world.description: Vietnam’s party chief and newly appointed president, To Lam, is set to meet Xi Jinping in China this week, seeking to shore up energy security as the war in Iran raises global supply risks and pushes Hanoi to deepen cooperation with its largest trading partner.

world.title: Mark Carney secures majority government in Canada after special election win
world.description: Carney’s Liberals will now be able to pass legislation without the support of opposition parties – and govern until 2029

world.title: Carney secures Liberal majority government after special election wins
world.description: After a wave of defections to the Liberals from opposition parties, projected special election victories help solidify the Canadian PM's hold on power.

world.title: Same road, different rules: How Malaysia’s fuel crisis exposed its leaders’ double standards
world.description: Fuel prices have climbed worldwide since USA and Israel struck Iran, triggering disruptions in traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. Yet in Malaysia, much of the political anger has been directed squarely at PM Anwar, a convenient target in a crisis shaped far beyond Putrajaya’s control.

world.title: Benin's finance minister wins presidential election with 94%
world.description: According to provisional results, Romuald Wadagni, the election favorite, won a landslide victory in Benin's presidential election.

world.title: Trump deletes social media post depicting himself as Jesus after outcry from religious leaders
world.description: He denies he was trying to look like Jesus Christ, saying “I thought it was me as a doctor and had to do Red Cross”. Read more at straitstimes.com.

world.title: Shanghai Hongqiao CBD to woo more MNCs
world.description: Shanghai Hongqiao International Central Business District, a portal for China's deepened two-way opening-up, will further improve its strength in commerce, exhibitions, traffic and technological innovation to prepare for high-quality development during the 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-30) period, said officials and company executives.

world.title: Trade shifts, but ties stay substantial, forum hears
world.description: Neither China nor the United States is seeking a complete decoupling, says Sarah Beran, a former US diplomat, during discussions on China-US trade relations at the 2026 China Town Hall program.

business.title: BYD Shenzhen factory fire hits scrapped vehicle garage, blaze contained, no casualties
business.description: A fire at BYD’s Shenzhen Pingshan facility was contained with no injuries, affecting a garage used for test vehicles.

science.description: A recorded image from space highlights a rare optical effect over Lake Iro in Chad and draws attention again to a geological structure that is currently under scientific analysis in the Sahel region of Africa. An image taken by an astronaut aboard the International Space Station showed Lake Iro in southern Chad, with [...]

science.title: Lunar soil bears proof of organic matter
science.description: An international research team has identified nitrogen-bearing organic compounds on the surface of moon soil for the first time, providing a new window into how the building blocks of life may have traveled through the early solar system.

science.title: Exoplanet Host Star Shares Elemental Traits with Its Hot Jupiter
science.description: An ultra-hot Jupiter exoplanet orbiting a nearby star gave scientists using the Gemini South telescope a look at how both a star and its hot planet can have similar chemical compositions. The team, led by Arizona State University graduate student Jorge Antonio Sanchez, took spectra of the planet, called WASP-189b, using the Immersion Grating Infrared Spectrograph instrument. The observations measured the abundance of magnesium compared to silicon in the hot planet's atmosphere and allowed the team to compare it to the makeup of its parent star.

science.title: Simulations reveal cold, dusty reality of galaxy formation
science.description: A realistic picture of how galaxies formed and evolved from the first billion years after the Big Bang to the present day has been revealed.

science.title: As Artemis II is celebrated, the world faces hard questions about US leadership in space

science.title: Asia's largest gamma-ray telescope, MACE, explores the high-energy cosmos
science.description: Perched 4.3 kilometres above sea level in the high-altitude desert of Ladakh, the MACE telescope is capturing fleeting flashes of light to uncover the secrets of the most extreme and energetic phenomena in the universe. | Bhabha Atomic Research Centre,MACE telescope,Astronomy,gamma rays | Research News by "Research Matters"
